findings.message: The recommended `CART_STORE_PROVIDER=prisma` activation is incomplete. `createCartStore()` only types `backend` as `"memory" | "redis" | "cloudflare"` and `loadCoreEnv()` currently validates `CART_STORE_PROVIDER` against the same enum, so setting `prisma` in `wrangler.toml` would fail env parsing / cart-store initialization unless the config schema and backend union are updated too.

The analysis still misstates the cart problem as filesystem-backed state that makes checkout deterministically fail. The cart path uses `MemoryCartStore` backed by an isolate-local `Map`, so the real issue is nondeterministic cross-isolate loss, not `fs` incompatibility or guaranteed `"Cart is empty"` on every checkout.

The document still treats removing `export const runtime = "nodejs"` as a required compatibility step, but the repo evidence only shows these routes are already deployed through OpenNext to a Worker with `nodejs_compat` and explicitly use `nodejs` today. The proven blocker is the fs-based idempotency store, not the runtime declaration itself.

The handoff still understates the migration surface as "all 8 exported functions." `checkoutIdempotency.server.ts` exports 10 functions, and not all I/O paths go through `withStoreLock`, so this count can cause the plan to omit part of the existing contract.
